title: Paper Goods Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend paper goods machines that perform a variety of functions, such as converting, sawing, corrugating, banding, wrapping, boxing, stitching, forming, or sealing paper or paperboard sheets into products.

tasks.text: Examine completed work to detect defects and verify conformance to work orders, and adjust machinery as necessary to correct production problems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe operation of various machines to detect and correct machine malfunctions such as improper forming, glue flow, or pasteboard tension.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start machines and move controls to regulate tension on pressure rolls, to synchronize speed of machine components, and to adjust temperatures of glue or paraffin.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Disassemble machines to maintain, repair, or replace broken or worn parts, using hand or power tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install attachments to machines for gluing, folding, printing, or cutting.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut products to specified dimensions, using hand or power cutters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place rolls of paper or cardboard on machine feed tracks, and thread paper through gluing, coating, and slitting rollers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor finished cartons as they drop from forming machines into rotating hoppers and into gravity feed chutes to prevent jamming.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Adjust guide assemblies, forming bars, and folding mechanisms according to specifications, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure, space, and set saw blades, cutters, and perforators, according to product specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fill glue and paraffin reservoirs, and position rollers to dispense glue onto paperboard.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Stamp products with information such as dates, using hand stamps or automatic stamping devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove finished cores, and stack or place them on conveyors for transfer to other work areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lift tote boxes of finished cartons, and dump cartons into feed hoppers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
